bullets: SEA PP yarn/injection -40 to 1,200 CFR; block copo -80 to 1,210.
Sabic copo finalized 1,200 CIF, dipping below that ex-3% tax.
Platts: SEA HDPE -20 to 1,290-1,300, LLDPE -20 to 1,250, LDPE -20 to 1,500.
Korean offers undercut: Lotte Hyundai HDPE finalized 1,180; SK 1,150-1,170.
Domestic Chinese LLDPE hit a market-low 31,500; Sibur PE down 400-700.
Formosa July to VN at 780 CIF (5% tax); AGC July offer expected mid-week.
Sinopec oil-based S-1000 to 720 CIF Hai Phong / 735 HCMC, below cost.
Coal-based SG-5 Erdos quoted to 21,600 (21,000 in volume) in the market.

editorial.changedNote: Broad declines; PP and PE led lower

summary: Asian PE kept falling, with SEA down most on rain-hit demand and improving Korean supply.
Korean offers undercut the market: Lotte Hyundai HDPE finalized 1,180, SK 1,150-1,170 CIF.
Domestic sellers raced to cut, with Chinese LLDPE hitting a market-low 31,500 VND/kg.
paras: Per Platts, NEA HDPE Film/yarn/injection/blow -10, LLDPE and LDPE -15. SEA HDPE -20 to 1,290-1,300 CFR, LLDPE -20 to 1,250, mC6 -20 to 1,400, LDPE -20 to 1,500.
Asian PE kept declining, SEA most sharply, on weak rain-hit demand and improving supply, especially more Korean offers back to SEA. The short-term outlook stays soft.

On June 11, Iran said it would fully freeze Strait of Hormuz operations, denting sentiment, though many saw no immediate PE impact. Malaysia PRefChem plans to restart HDPE (400,000 t/yr) and LLDPE (350,000 t/yr) in late June after a 3-month outage.

In Vietnam, Sabic-Tianjin LLDPE 222WT offered at 1,230 CIF Hai Phong (up 10 after a 50 drop), with limited response. Lotte Hyundai LDPE LF512 at 1,470; HDPE Film FL7000 and yarn MF5000 both 1,270, but one HDPE Film finalized at 1,180 (90 below offer). SK HDPE Film/yarn at 1,200, with finals at 1,150-1,170.

Lotte Titan re-offered July PE: 260GG -40 to 1,500, injection 300YY -60 to 1,560; HDPE Film 1,280, blow 1,330. Sibur cut LLDPE to 34,200 (-400), HDPE Film 36,500 (-500), HDPE yarn 35,600 (-700), HDPE injection 35,200-35,500 (-500).

LSP sold out HDPE Film and held PE offers flat a 4th week while distributors sold lower. In the North, Lotte Titan 260GG to 49,000; Sabic/Borouge LDPE to 48,000. Qamar F1 finalized 36,500 ex-VAT; commercial 38,000-38,300 (down 1,500-2,000 vs early May). Sabic 218WJ offered 35,500; Chinese LLDPE hit a low 31,500.

summary: SEA PP fell hardest (yarn/injection -40, block copo -80) while NEA held mostly flat.
Sabic copo finalized a competitive 1,200 CIF, dipping below that excluding 3% tax.
Domestic dropped sharply: NSRP 114G 33,400-33,600 and Sibur cut yarn/inj 1,300-1,600.
paras: Per Platts on June 11, NEA PP yarn/injection/block copo flat, BOPP and IPP -10. SEA yarn/injection -40 to 1,200 CFR, BOPP -50 to 1,220, IPP Film -20 to 1,270, block copo -80 to 1,210 (Vietnam 30-40 lower, the regional low).

Asian PP moved sideways to lower, SEA sharpest. Chinese supply stayed soft in maintenance, holding domestic prices; FOB PP yarn/injection ~1,220 but uncompetitive for Vietnam. Some Chinese traders even looked to import Vietnamese PP.

Sabic PP was highly competitive, copo outshining Chinese and Korean goods. Late last week Sabic offered PP homo 1,200 CIF and copo 1,230; by mid-week copo finalized 1,200 CIF, with talk of finals below 1,200 excluding 3% tax.

LSP, after ~3 weeks shut, held PP offers flat a 4th week while distributors sold much lower. Domestic PP homo supply stayed ample; Chinese supply lost ground while Korean PP returned, intensifying competition. Vietnamese majors pushed PP exports to SEA and China.
Sibur offered PP yarn/injection and non-woven 33,900-34,300 (down 1,300-1,600) and copo 34,800 (down 700) at Hai Phong. NSRP 114G commonly 33,400-33,600 North; BSR T3034 to 35,000 both regions.

In the North, Lotte PP random copo J560s to 37,000 (down up to 8,000 vs mid-May) as Korean off-grade copo fell to 30,500-31,500. In the South, copo commonly 36,000-38,000; Lotte coating M9600 finalized as low as 46,000; Hyosung Vina random 46,000-47,000.

summary: Asian PVC fell sharply (China and SEA both to 760 CFR) on weak demand and lower July offers.
Formosa July to Vietnam landed at 780 CIF; AGC July offer expected mid-week, likely lower.
Sinopec oil-based S-1000 to 720 CIF Hai Phong, below the 750-800 China production cost.
paras: Per Platts, China PVC -90 to 760 CFR; SEA -40 to 760 CFR. Prices fell on weak demand and lower July offers, led by Formosa Taiwan cutting July by 115-120 vs last month. High China freight also slowed exports.
Some Vietnamese customers received Formosa July at 780 CIF Hai Phong (5% tax), with limited interest, mainly from buyers already taking other FPC goods.
A major distributor offered Sinopec oil-based S-1000 at 720 CIF Hai Phong / 735 HCMC (incl. CIC), down ~100-120 vs mid-May. With China oil-based cost ~750-800, makers are selling at a loss.
AGC Group is expected to offer July PVC to Vietnam mid-week, likely below Formosa.
Domestically, a distributor held ~500 tons of coal-based SG-5 Erdos at 21,600 (down 400 vs last week low), with ~21,000 possible in volume; the coal-based floor keeps breaking lower.
Oil-based fell faster: some Chinese oil-based to 22,500, narrowing the gap to coal-based; other oil-based grades stayed under heavy pressure.
